topStory.text: Communities across the United States have blocked or delayed more than $130 billion in AI data centers in the first six months of 2026, with projects from Google, Amazon, and other major companies facing opposition over power grid strain, water consumption, and rising electricity costs, according to industry analysis.

feed.text: Virginia's first-in-the-nation data-center electricity tax took effect July 1 — $0.011 per kWh on all power consumed by data centers, including self-generated and behind-the-meter supply — expected to raise about $600M a year and adding roughly 10% to a facility's effective power rate (nearly $100M annually for a 1 GW campus).

FERC issued show-cause orders to all six US grid operators, giving each 60 days to justify or rewrite their tariffs for connecting data centers and other gigawatt-scale loads — covering co-location, behind-the-meter generation, flexible-load service and cost-shift protections — plus 30 days to report how they will ensure adequate generation for large loads.

Meta unveiled its first data center in Canada — a CA$13B (~US$10B), gigawatt-scale AI campus on 1,750 acres in Sturgeon County, Alberta, to be powered by a new natural-gas plant from a Pembina Pipeline-led consortium — underscoring how US hyperscalers are reaching across the border for land and power amid domestic grid constraints.

JLL's latest North America report shows the availability crunch deepening — data-center vacancy at historic lows (about 0.3% in Northern Virginia), more than 35 GW under construction across the region, and roughly 80% of that pipeline pre-leased before it comes online.

A wave of mid-2026 analyses now frames power availability — not capital or chips — as the binding constraint on the US AI buildout: S&P Global projects data centers' share of US peak-summer electricity demand rising from about 4.1% in 2025 to 5.3% in 2026, with reliability risk flagged as elevated across the Mid-Atlantic, Midcontinent and Northwest.

Texas's ERCOT 'Batch Zero' process — approved by state regulators to study large loads of 75 MW or more as a single cohort — hits its first election deadlines on July 10 and July 24, even as ERCOT discloses it is now tracking more than 438 GW of large-load interconnection requests, roughly 89% of it tied to data centers.

Anthropic signed a 20-year, ~$19B lease with TeraWulf for up to 401 MW of AI compute at the Justified Data campus in Hawesville, Kentucky — among the largest single-tenant AI data-center leases disclosed to date, as AI labs race to lock up long-duration capacity ahead of first operations in late 2027.

PJM's heat-wave demand surged to about 163 GW on July 2 — just short of the 2006 all-time record of 165,563 MW — after a July 1 peak of 161,910 MW, the second-highest load in the grid's history, with DOE's first-ever data-center curtailment orders in effect through July 3.
